observations.description: An array of observation contents associated with the entity

tools_detail.name: create_relations

to.description: The name of the entity where the relation ends

contents.description: An array of observation contents to add

tools_detail.name: delete_entities
tools_detail.description: Delete multiple entities and their associated relations from the knowledge graph

entityNames.description: An array of entity names to delete

observations.description: An array of observations to delete

from.description: The name of the entity where the relation starts

to.description: The name of the entity where the relation ends

observations.description: An array of observation contents associated with the entity

from.description: The name of the entity where the relation starts

to.description: The name of the entity where the relation ends

tools_detail.name: open_nodes
tools_detail.description: Open specific nodes in the knowledge graph by their names

observations.description: An array of observation contents associated with the entity

from.description: The name of the entity where the relation starts

to.description: The name of the entity where the relation ends

A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on,

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

tools_detail.description: Returns all environment variables, helpful for debugging MCP server configuration

data.description: URL or data URI of the file content to compress

outputType.description: How the resulting gzipped file should be returned. 'resourceLink' returns a link to a resource that can be read later, 'resource' returns a full resource object.

duration.description: Duration of the operation in seconds

steps.description: Number of steps in the operation

tools_detail.name: browser_close
tools_detail.description: Close the page

tools_detail.name: browser_resize
tools_detail.description: Resize the browser window

tools_detail.name: browser_console_messages
tools_detail.description: Returns all console messages

level.description: Level of the console messages to return. Each level includes the messages of more severe levels. Defaults to "info".

all.description: Return all console messages since the beginning of the session, not just since the last navigation. Defaults to false.

filename.description: Filename to save the console messages to. If not provided, messages are returned as text.

tools_detail.name: browser_handle_dialog
tools_detail.description: Handle a dialog

promptText.description: The text of the prompt in case of a prompt dialog.

tools_detail.name: browser_evaluate
tools_detail.description: Evaluate JavaScript expression on page or element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

function.description: () => { /* code */ } or (element) => { /* code */ } when element is provided

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, result is returned as text.

tools_detail.name: browser_file_upload
tools_detail.description: Upload one or multiple files

paths.description: The absolute paths to the files to upload. Can be single file or multiple files. If omitted, file chooser is cancelled.

tools_detail.name: browser_drop
tools_detail.description: Drop files or MIME-typed data onto an element, as if dragged from outside the page. At least one of "paths" or "data" must be provided.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

paths.description: Absolute paths to files to drop onto the element.

data.description: Data to drop, as a map of MIME type to string value (e.g. {"text/plain": "hello", "text/uri-list": "https://example.com"}).

tools_detail.name: browser_fill_form
tools_detail.description: Fill multiple form fields

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

value.description: Value to fill in the field. If the field is a checkbox, the value should be `true` or `false`. If the field is a combobox, the value should be the text of the option.

tools_detail.name: browser_press_key
tools_detail.description: Press a key on the keyboard

key.description: Name of the key to press or a character to generate, such as `ArrowLeft` or `a`

tools_detail.name: browser_type
tools_detail.description: Type text into editable element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

text.description: Text to type into the element

submit.description: Whether to submit entered text (press Enter after)

slowly.description: Whether to type one character at a time. Useful for triggering key handlers in the page. By default entire text is filled in at once.

tools_detail.name: browser_navigate
tools_detail.description: Navigate to a URL

tools_detail.name: browser_navigate_back
tools_detail.description: Go back to the previous page in the history

tools_detail.name: browser_network_requests
tools_detail.description: Returns a numbered list of network requests since loading the page. Use browser_network_request with the number to get full details.

static.description: Whether to include successful static resources like images, fonts, scripts, etc. Defaults to false.

filter.description: Only return requests whose URL matches this regexp (e.g. "/api/.*user").

filename.description: Filename to save the network requests to. If not provided, requests are returned as text.

tools_detail.name: browser_network_request
tools_detail.description: Returns full details (headers and body) of a single network request, or a single part if `part` is set. Use the number from browser_network_requests.

index.description: 1-based index of the request, as printed by browser_network_requests.

part.description: Return only this part of the request. Omit to return full details.

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, output is returned as text.

tools_detail.name: browser_run_code_unsafe
tools_detail.description: Run a Playwright code snippet. Unsafe: executes arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright server process and is RCE-equivalent.

code.description: A JavaScript function containing Playwright code to execute. It will be invoked with a single argument, page, which you can use for any page interaction. For example: `async (page) => { await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click(); return await page.title(); }`

filename.description: Load code from the specified file. If both code and filename are provided, code will be ignored.

tools_detail.name: browser_take_screenshot
tools_detail.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

type.description: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

fullPage.description: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

tools_detail.name: browser_snapshot
tools_detail.description: Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

filename.description: Save snapshot to markdown file instead of returning it in the response.

depth.description: Limit the depth of the snapshot tree

boxes.description: Include each element's bounding box as [box=x,y,width,height] in the snapshot. Coordinates are viewport-relative, in CSS pixels (Element.getBoundingClientRect)

tools_detail.name: browser_click
tools_detail.description: Perform click on a web page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

doubleClick.description: Whether to perform a double click instead of a single click

button.description: Button to click, defaults to left

tools_detail.name: browser_drag
tools_detail.description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

tools_detail.name: browser_hover
tools_detail.description: Hover over element on page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

tools_detail.name: browser_select_option
tools_detail.description: Select an option in a dropdown

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

values.description: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

tools_detail.name: browser_tabs
tools_detail.description: List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

index.description: Tab index, used for close/select. If omitted for close, current tab is closed.

url.description: URL to navigate to in the new tab, used for new.

tools_detail.name: browser_wait_for
tools_detail.description: Wait for text to appear or disappear or a specified time to pass

time.description: The time to wait in seconds

textGone.description: The text to wait for to disappear

tools_detail.name: read_file
tools_detail.description: Read the complete contents of a file as text. DEPRECATED: Use read_text_file instead.

tail.description: If provided, returns only the last N lines of the file

head.description: If provided, returns only the first N lines of the file

tools_detail.name: read_text_file
tools_detail.description: Read the complete contents of a file from the file system as text. Handles various text encodings and provides detailed error messages if the file cannot be read. Use this tool when you need to examin

tail.description: If provided, returns only the last N lines of the file

head.description: If provided, returns only the first N lines of the file

tools_detail.name: read_media_file
tools_detail.description: Read an image or audio file. Returns the base64 encoded data and MIME type. Only works within allowed directories.

tools_detail.name: read_multiple_files
tools_detail.description: Read the contents of multiple files simultaneously. This is more efficient than reading files one by one when you need to analyze or compare multiple files. Each file's content is returned with its pa

paths.description: Array of file paths to read. Each path must be a string pointing to a valid file within allowed directories.

tools_detail.name: write_file
tools_detail.description: Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file with new content. Use with caution as it will overwrite existing files without warning. Handles text content with proper encoding. Only works

tools_detail.name: edit_file
tools_detail.description: Make line-based edits to a text file. Each edit replaces exact line sequences with new content. Returns a git-style diff showing the changes made. Only works within allowed directories.

oldText.description: Text to search for - must match exactly

dryRun.description: Preview changes using git-style diff format

tools_detail.name: create_directory
tools_detail.description: Create a new directory or ensure a directory exists. Can create multiple nested directories in one operation. If the directory already exists, this operation will succeed silently. Perfect for setting

tools_detail.name: list_directory
tools_detail.description: Get a detailed listing of all files and directories in a specified path. Results clearly distinguish between files and directories with [FILE] and [DIR] prefixes. This tool is essential for understand

tools_detail.name: list_directory_with_sizes
tools_detail.description: Get a detailed listing of all files and directories in a specified path, including sizes. Results clearly distinguish between files and directories with [FILE] and [DIR] prefixes. This tool is useful

sortBy.description: Sort entries by name or size

tools_detail.name: directory_tree
tools_detail.description: Get a recursive tree view of files and directories as a JSON structure. Each entry includes 'name', 'type' (file/directory), and 'children' for directories. Files have no children array, while directo

tools_detail.name: move_file
tools_detail.description: Move or rename files and directories. Can move files between directories and rename them in a single operation. If the destination exists, the operation will fail. Works across different directories a

tools_detail.name: search_files
tools_detail.description: Recursively search for files and directories matching a pattern. The patterns should be glob-style patterns that match paths relative to the working directory. Use pattern like '*.ext' to match files

tools_detail.name: get_file_info
tools_detail.description: Retrieve detailed metadata about a file or directory. Returns comprehensive information including size, creation time, last modified time, permissions, and type. This tool is perfect for understanding

tools_detail.name: list_allowed_directories
tools_detail.description: Returns the list of directories that this server is allowed to access. Subdirectories within these allowed directories are also accessible. Use this to understand which directories and their nested pa
